_comment: Tasks for MiOS. Every task runs under PowerShell on Windows — no bash, no WSL. The original bash-heavy tasks caused Windows to spawn external WSL popups because bare `bash` on Win11 with WSL installed routes through wsl.exe. Each task below uses PowerShell-native patterns or explicit `wsl -- <cmd>` invocations that stay in the integrated terminal. If you need to run bash tasks inside WSL, use `wsl -e bash -c '...'` explicitly; that keeps the output embedded.

tasks.label: Lint: ALL (mirrors pr-lint.yml)
args: $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; Write-Host '=== shellcheck ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File -Filter '*.sh' | Where-Object { $_.FullName -notmatch '\\\.git\\' } | ForEach-Object { shellcheck -S warning $_.FullName }; Write-Host '=== hadolint ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan; hadolint Containerfile; Write-Host '=== yamllint ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan; yamllint .github/workflows/ renovate.json image-versions.yml; Write-Host '=== TOML ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File -Filter '*.toml' | Where-Object { $_.FullName -notmatch '\\\.git\\' } | ForEach-Object { taplo check $_.FullName }; Write-Host 'ALL PASS' -ForegroundColor Green

tasks.label: Smoke: Ephemeral QEMU boot test (runs inside WSL)
tasks._comment: Explicit wsl -e invocation stays in the integrated terminal. bare `bash` spawns external WSL windows; `wsl -e` does not.
